name: Safe Passage Transportation
recent_news: Operating as Indiana Medicaid NEMT provider; no recent M&A or growth news identified.
fit_notes: NEMT/wheelchair provider — not a school bus contractor. Limited fit for Summit's K-12 yellow-bus tuck-in thesis; possible adjacency only if Summit expands into SPED/medical transport.

name: Willamette Valley Transport
districts_or_customers: Physically and developmentally disabled community in Portland, Salem, Eugene Oregon
recent_news: Grew from single van in 2000 to 70+ vehicles across three Oregon offices (Portland/Salem/Eugene); founder still listed as President.
fit_notes: Pure NEMT/disabled-adult transport — not K-12 school bus. Founder-led 25+ years (succession adjacent) but service mix outside Summit's core yellow-bus thesis.

name: Cove Taxi
districts_or_customers: Copperas Cove TX/Central Texas general public; some wheelchair/special-needs trips
recent_news: Closed permanently September 30, 2022 after 26 years; owners Clarence Page and family retired (not Uber/Lyft-driven per owner).
fit_notes: DEFUNCT — ceased operations 9/30/2022. Not a viable target. Was a taxi/wheelchair service, never a school bus operator. Remove from pipeline.

name: Reliant Transportation
districts_or_customers: Anchorage School District (operates ~158 buses, ~2/3 of district routes, incl. Chugiak/Eagle River)
NYC DOE (~600 buses via Reliant brand)
recent_news: Subsidiary of MV Transportation (formerly USA United Fleet, acquired by MV out of bankruptcy ~2018). MV-owned brand operating large multi-district school contracts including ASD.
website: https://www.mvtransit.com/ (parent); local ops via ASD contract
fit_notes: NOT A TUCK-IN TARGET — owned by MV Transportation, a top-10 national school bus operator. Strategic-owned/PE-backed platform; competitor scale, not a target. Remove from independent-operator universe.

name: Five Star Transport
owner_or_parent: Student Transportation of America (STA) Family of Companies
districts_or_customers: 15 school districts across Western MA and Cape Cod (Hadley/Amherst terminal at 325 Rocky Hill Rd)
recent_news: Family-run since 1989, transitioned to 2nd generation; now operates as a member of Student Transportation of America Family of Companies (STA acquired). Operates 250+ buses across 7 terminals.
fit_notes: NOT A TUCK-IN TARGET — already acquired by Student Transportation of America (STA), a top-3 national school bus platform (PE-owned by CDPQ). Direct Summit competitor; remove from independent-operator universe.
